title: Issue #307: canonical same-turn stage-unblock mechanism
context: The stages block message points at scripts/se-mark.ts which is never published (package files excludes scripts/) nor seeded; inline paqad:stage markers only parse at Stop so the first turn can never unblock itself; the sentinel write itself is blocked by both agent-entry-gate and the stages gate (turn-one deadlock). All options include: exempting .paqad/.agent-entry-loaded from both gates, fixing every message/doc to reference only mechanisms that exist, and a packaging-truth guardrail test suite with 100% coverage on the stages gate.

options.label: Parse paqad:stage markers at the pre-mutation seam too (capability-gate already gets transcript_path) so markers emitted earlier in the SAME turn clear the block; plus add CLI verb 'paqad-ai stage start|end <stage>' as the shell path; drop the se-mark.ts reference from messages
Add CLI verb 'paqad-ai stage start|end <stage>' and repoint the block message + bootstrap to it; markers stay Stop-only (same-turn unblock via shell only)
Publish se-mark and seed it into every onboarded project's scripts/ at onboarding so the current message becomes true (churns project dirs, against the light-dir principle)

rationale: Same-turn unblock must not depend on shell access; capability-gate already receives transcript_path at PreToolUse. CLI verb added as the shell-path complement. Chosen by Haider 2026-07-07.
